key_points: A quadratic equation is an equation of degree 2
Standard form of a quadratic equation is ax² + bx + c = 0, where a ≠ 0
A quadratic equation can have at most two roots
Quadratic equations can be solved by factorisation or quadratic formula
The graph of a quadratic equation is a parabola
exam_tips: If D > 0, roots are real and distinct
If D = 0, roots are real and equal
If D < 0, roots are not real
Roots of quadratic equation are x-intercepts of the graph
